name: Babysitter
description: Orchestrate complex multi-step AI workflows with quality gates, breakpoints, and event sourcing

chatParticipants.name: babysitter
chatParticipants.description: Orchestrate complex multi-step workflows with quality gates, breakpoints, and resumable event-sourced runs

commands.name: implement
commands.description: Start a new orchestrated implementation run (TDD, multi-phase pipelines, quality convergence)

commands.name: resume
commands.description: Resume a paused or interrupted run from its last checkpoint

commands.name: status
commands.description: Check the status of a run, including current phase, pending tasks, and quality score

commands.name: list
commands.description: List all runs in the current workspace with their statuses

commands.name: processes
commands.description: List available process definitions from .a5c/processes/ and the built-in process library

commands.name: help
commands.description: Show usage examples, available process templates, and orchestration patterns

skills.name: create-process
skills.description: Create a new babysitter process definition file in .a5c/processes/

name.description: Process identifier (used as processId and filename)

description.description: Human-readable description of what the process does

methodology.description: Optional: methodology template to base the process on (tdd, scrum, kanban, bdd, ddd, xp, etc.)

qualityTarget.description: Optional: minimum quality score threshold (0-100, default 80)

skills.name: list-processes
skills.description: List all available process definitions from .a5c/processes/ and the built-in process library

category.description: Optional: filter by category (gsd, methodologies, specializations)

skills.name: dispatch-run
skills.description: Create and start a new orchestration run using the babysitter SDK

processId.description: ID of the process definition to use

entry.description: Path to the process entry file (e.g. .a5c/processes/my-process.js#process)

inputs.description: Process inputs as a JSON object

inputsFile.description: Optional: path to a JSON file containing process inputs

skills.name: check-status
skills.description: Check the status of an existing run, including pending tasks, quality scores, and journal events

runId.description: Run ID or path to the run directory under .a5c/runs/

showEvents.description: Whether to include journal events in the response (default: false)

skills.name: run-iterate
skills.description: Trigger a new orchestration iteration for an existing run, processing pending effects and advancing the workflow

runId.description: Run ID or path to the run directory under .a5c/runs/

json.description: Return iteration result as structured JSON (default: false)

hooks.description: Fired when a babysitter run is created and begins executing
Fired when a run finishes successfully with a final output
Fired when a run terminates with an unrecoverable error
Fired when an effect (task, breakpoint, sleep) is dispatched by the process
Fired when a pending effect is resolved and its result posted to the run
Fired when the process hits a ctx.breakpoint() and requires human approval to continue
Fired at the start of each orchestration iteration (replay cycle)
Fired at the end of each orchestration iteration with the outcome

instructions.description: Babysitter orchestration guidance for Copilot chat
